noteP1: We picked fifteen companies from our portfolio that map to the priorities of AIG's data organization: frontier AI and the infrastructure to run it economically, the data foundations underneath, and the security and developer tooling that let both move to production.
noteP2: Each card opens to a brief and the angle we think fits AIG best, and you can filter by category. Our full portfolio of 150+ active companies is linked in the top right. Whenever it's convenient, send back the ones worth a closer look.
portfolioIntro: Every company here works at the infrastructure layer, across AI, data, security, and developer tooling, with an angle on where it fits AIG's agenda. Filter by category, then expand any card for the brief.

anthropic.pull: AIG already has Anthropic in its strategic AI orbit; the next question is how broadly Claude can support the operating model behind AIG Assist. The opportunity spans underwriting submissions, policy language, claims files, loss histories and broker correspondence, where long-context reasoning and controlled agents can help underwriters, claims professionals, actuaries and distribution teams move faster without obscuring judgment. Claude Code also gives engineering teams a common assistant across underwriting, claims, pricing and broker-service applications. The expansion case is strongest when model policies, human review and evidence capture are treated as part of the workflow, preserving model transparency, privacy, auditability and regulated decision accountability as AIG scales from targeted copilots to an enterprise AI layer.
cases: Reason across underwriting submissions, policy language, claims files, loss histories and broker correspondence without forcing teams into a separate knowledge system.
Use Claude Code across underwriting, claims, pricing and broker-service applications and the integration services behind them.
Keep human review, model policy and audit evidence explicit in high-consequence workflows.

wispr.pull: For AIG, the fastest route to better operational data may be improving how people enter it. Wispr Flow sits beneath existing software as a voice-first input layer, allowing underwriters, claims professionals, actuaries and distribution teams to dictate loss-adjuster interviews, claim notes, broker conversations and field inspections into the systems already in use. Spoken observations become usable text at the speed of the work, including on mobile. That would reduce documentation friction across underwriting, claims, pricing and broker-service applications, preserve more of the rationale behind decisions and create a higher-quality record for analytics, handoffs and agentic workflows.
cases: Turn loss-adjuster interviews, claim notes, broker conversations and field inspections into structured entries while the context is still fresh.
Work inside underwriting, claims, pricing and broker-service applications rather than creating another application to manage.
Create a richer decision trail for handoffs, analytics and supervised agents.

browserbase.pull: The API surface around AIG does not cover every workflow. Teams still depend on broker portals, third-party risk sites, regulatory filing systems and legacy web tools, creating manual handoffs and fragile scripts. Browserbase gives agents a managed browser layer that can navigate those tools, preserve session context and produce an observable execution trail. It would be useful both for automating bounded tasks and for continuously testing underwriting, claims, pricing and broker-service applications across real browser conditions. For the data organization, that means extending automation into the web estate while maintaining governance around credentials, data access and model transparency, privacy, auditability and regulated decision accountability.
cases: Automate bounded tasks across broker portals, third-party risk sites, regulatory filing systems and legacy web tools without waiting for every system to expose an API.
Run repeatable browser tests across underwriting, claims, pricing and broker-service applications before customer- or employee-facing releases.
Centralize sessions, credentials and execution logs for governed agent activity.

coder.pull: The teams maintaining underwriting, claims, pricing and broker-service applications need fast access to complex toolchains, but local workstations make environments inconsistent and put source code, credentials and regulated data closer to the edge. Coder matters because it moves developer workspaces into centrally governed cloud environments. For AIG, that can standardize onboarding, reproduce builds and apply security controls to the place where engineers work, not only to the pipeline after they commit. That matters because the current agenda—scaling agentic AI in underwriting and claims while building an enterprise ontology that can contextualize the business in real time—requires more teams to gain approved access to modern AI, data and platform tooling.
cases: Standardize cloud workspaces for teams building underwriting, claims, pricing and broker-service applications.
Keep source code, credentials and approved AI tooling inside controlled infrastructure.
Reduce onboarding and environment drift across distributed, acquired or specialized teams.

token.pull: As AIG adds agents to submission-to-bind and notice-to-settlement workflows, every useful workflow introduces a non-human principal with credentials, permissions and an owner—or an ownership gap. Token Security matters because it treats those identities as a distinct governance domain rather than an extension of employee access. The platform can continuously find service accounts, APIs, workflow bots and underwriting or claims agents, govern their lifecycle and enforce access based on intended purpose. That would help AIG scale automation while keeping the blast radius around policy, exposure, pricing, claims and broker data narrow and the audit trail clear.
cases: Inventory service accounts, APIs, workflow bots and underwriting or claims agents and tie every credential to an accountable owner.
Constrain agent access to the specific policy, exposure, pricing, claims and broker data required for each workflow.
Preserve lifecycle and entitlement evidence for audits, regulators and internal risk review.

fal.pull: For AIG, multimodal AI can make submission-to-bind and notice-to-settlement workflows more visual, interactive and accessible. fal provides the infrastructure to run image, video, audio and voice models at production speed without requiring every product team to manage GPU serving. That could support damage imagery, inspection video and policyholder communications across internal tools and external experiences, with the application retaining control over data, approvals and user context. The strategic benefit is a reusable inference layer that lets teams test more ideas, standardize deployment and reserve custom infrastructure work for the workloads that truly require it.
cases: Embed image, video, audio or voice generation into new underwriting, claims and broker-service capabilities.
Prototype against multiple media models through one developer-facing inference layer.
Keep review, consent and brand or safety controls in the surrounding application workflow.

montecarlo.pull: AIG's data platform has to support its current agenda—scaling agentic AI in underwriting and claims while building an enterprise ontology that can contextualize the business in real time—and remain trusted continuously, not only after a dashboard or agent produces a bad answer. Monte Carlo can monitor policy, exposure, pricing, claims and broker data from ingestion through transformation, model and agent behavior, connecting a failure to the systems and teams affected. That gives underwriters, claims professionals, actuaries and distribution teams a faster way to isolate incidents and prevent recurrence. For the data organization, the value is a single trust plane across traditional analytics and emerging agentic workflows, reducing the gap between data quality, model reliability and business impact.
cases: Monitor policy, exposure, pricing, claims and broker data from source through dashboards, models and agent actions.
Trace incidents to the affected underwriting, claims, pricing and broker-service applications and accountable engineering teams.
Prevent silent data failures from undermining shorter cycle times without weakening underwriting discipline or claims controls.

tonic.pull: For AIG, better test data can improve both delivery speed and production safety. Tonic can create high-fidelity synthetic versions of policyholder, claimant, broker and loss data, allowing developers to exercise underwriting, claims, pricing and broker-service applications against realistic volumes and relationships without relying on raw production data. The same approach can support model training, fine-tuning and evaluation for document extraction, underwriting copilots, claims triage and risk agents. The strategic fit is strongest where data provisioning is slowed by model transparency, privacy, auditability and regulated decision accountability, yet inadequate test coverage would expose the organization to even greater operational or customer risk.
cases: Replace production copies of policyholder, claimant, broker and loss data in development and test environments.
Generate rare and edge-case scenarios for underwriting, claims, pricing and broker-service applications and AI evaluation.
Reduce privacy exposure while giving teams faster access to useful data.

netbox.pull: AIG depends on corporate, branch, partner-connected and multi-cloud infrastructure, but automation is only as reliable as the inventory and configuration data behind it. NetBox Labs matters as a source of truth for documenting, managing and automating IT and network infrastructure. It could give the data organization one current model of sites, devices, circuits, addresses and dependencies across that estate. That foundation makes changes to multi-cloud data, underwriting, claims and AI workloads safer, improves incident context and gives infrastructure agents a trusted state to read before they act. It replaces stale spreadsheets with governed infrastructure context and supports shorter cycle times without weakening underwriting discipline or claims controls.
cases: Create a maintained source of truth across corporate, branch, partner-connected and multi-cloud infrastructure.
Drive provisioning and change validation for multi-cloud data, underwriting, claims and AI workloads from intended state.
Ground infrastructure automation and agents in accurate topology and ownership data.

valar.pull: The economics of AIG's AI program will change as document extraction, underwriting copilots, claims triage and risk agents move from pilots to sustained volume. Valar is designed for that stage. It schedules and batches inference across available accelerators inside the enterprise VPC, improving throughput and cost per token while preserving the existing security boundary. For workloads involving policy, exposure, pricing, claims and broker data, that architecture keeps model traffic, logs and policy enforcement under AIG's control. The result is a production inference layer that advances shorter cycle times without weakening underwriting discipline or claims controls while preserving control over scale and data locality.
cases: Run document extraction, underwriting copilots, claims triage and risk agents inside AIG's own VPC and security boundary.
Increase usable accelerator capacity through scheduling, batching and routing.
Keep prompts, outputs, logs, models and policies under enterprise control.

echo.pull: AIG's underwriting, claims, analytics and agent applications are assembled from thousands of base images, libraries and packages that can introduce vulnerabilities before application code is written. Echo matters because it supplies trusted, continuously patched and hardened software dependencies, including FIPS-validated options, at the component layer. The data organization could use that foundation to reduce the vulnerability backlog across underwriting, claims, pricing and broker-service applications and give developers secure defaults without slowing delivery. The fit is especially strong where the risk profile around model transparency, privacy, auditability and regulated decision accountability makes end-of-pipeline remediation too late or too expensive.
cases: Standardize hardened dependencies across underwriting, claims, analytics and agent applications.
Remove known vulnerabilities before they enter application and agent build pipelines.
Use FIPS-validated options where stronger assurance is required.

act.pull: The cloud-security question for AIG is not only who has access; it is which application, service or agent can communicate with which other system. Act Security is built to answer that question continuously. By learning intended and observed pathways across multi-cloud data, underwriting, claims and AI workloads, the platform can identify unnecessary reachability and help teams lock it down with business context intact. That supports shorter cycle times without weakening underwriting discipline or claims controls by reducing lateral movement and configuration ambiguity while preserving the connections its people and production workflows genuinely need.
cases: Map intended and actual communication across multi-cloud data, underwriting, claims and AI workloads.
Create least-privilege perimeters around underwriting, claims, pricing and broker-service applications and their data services.
Reduce lateral-movement risk without deploying another host agent.

clover.pull: AIG's technology agenda spans new underwriting, claims and broker-service capabilities, each with different data, identity and operational assumptions. Clover Security gives product-security teams a way to review that intent directly from planning and design materials instead of waiting for a scan at the end. It can flag missing requirements, route decisions to accountable owners and keep the rationale attached to the work item. That supports shorter cycle times without weakening underwriting discipline or claims controls by making secure design part of the normal delivery motion across underwriting, claims, pricing and broker-service applications, not a separate process teams learn to avoid.
cases: Review new underwriting, claims and broker-service capabilities while requirements and architecture are still being shaped.
Map security decisions back to tickets, design documents and accountable owners.
Reduce late-stage rework across underwriting, claims, pricing and broker-service applications without adding another approval queue.

drata.pull: AIG's current agenda—scaling agentic AI in underwriting and claims while building an enterprise ontology that can contextualize the business in real time—means trust has to keep pace with releases, integrations and agent adoption. Drata's platform can continuously monitor the control environment around underwriting, claims, pricing and broker-service applications, organize evidence and connect risk to accountable owners. That helps the data organization move away from spreadsheet-driven point-in-time compliance toward an operating model in which readiness is always visible. For an organization exposed to model transparency, privacy, auditability and regulated decision accountability, that also improves the speed and consistency of customer, regulator and partner assurance.
cases: Continuously map controls and evidence across underwriting, claims, pricing and broker-service applications and multi-cloud data, underwriting, claims and AI workloads.
Reuse assurance work across insurance regulation, model risk, privacy, audit and third-party assurance instead of rebuilding it for every review.
Give leaders a current view of control drift, ownership and third-party risk.

orca.pull: The cloud-security problem at AIG is not a shortage of findings; it is knowing which combinations of exposure, identity, vulnerability and data actually matter across multi-cloud data, underwriting, claims and AI workloads. Orca Security connects those signals in one agentless platform and guides remediation by business context. For the data organization, that can reduce coverage gaps across underwriting, claims, pricing and broker-service applications and emerging AI workloads while avoiding host-agent overhead. The value is a clearer, faster path from a cloud risk to the owner and action required to protect submission-to-bind and notice-to-settlement workflows.
cases: Gain agentless visibility across AIG's cloud estate and emerging AI workloads.
Prioritize attack paths into sensitive AIG data and critical applications.
Unify posture, workload, data, vulnerability and compliance remediation in one view.
